episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: This morning the story was the repricing of the frontier — challengers attacking from below, flagships arriving gated from above.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday the market bought the disinflation story on the strength of a soft June CPI print. Today it got three answers back — from the American consumer, from China, and from the banks — and none of them fit neatly inside that trade.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: This morning the story was the float wars — who keeps the yield on stablecoin reserves.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: This morning the frame was chokepoints — Hormuz and the grid. Tonight the frame is what a great power does when its growth model falters: it tightens its grip on the things the rest of the world cannot make without it.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: This morning I told you TSMC was taking the stand. Tonight we have the testimony, and it was emphatic — but the more interesting story of the day is the one running underneath it: while the leading edge printed a record, the commodity end of the chip market is being invaded, and the invasion has a flag on it.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: This morning we covered the agent's arrival as a line item — coding tools, voice, enterprise plumbing. Tonight the through-line is the next turn of that screw: the products reshaping where AI actually lives.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: This morning we covered the earthquake — IBM's worst trading day on record and the repricing of software as the funding source for the AI buildout.

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: This morning we mapped who owns the engine — frontier labs going direct, pharma building sovereign models, vendors converting to subscriptions. Tonight the question shifts from ownership to adjudication: who decides whether any of it works.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: One fact organizes almost everything reported across today's episodes: TSMC's record quarter. Profit up 77 percent, capex confirmed at the largest budget in the company's history, and management attributing it all to agentic AI driving compute requirements per task structurally higher.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 17, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday, Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, an open-weight model live now on Kimi Work, Kimi Code, and the Kimi API.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 17, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the number that matters most this week: global semiconductor stocks have shed roughly $3.3 trillion in market value since June 22, nearing bear-market territory. This is the unwind of a trade that had run up well over 100% in the prior twelve months, so there is a lot of air beneath these stocks.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 17, 2026
episodes.summary: The real move in digital money this week isn't Bitcoin's price. It's a two-sided attack on the reserve-float income that built the two biggest listed stablecoin businesses, Circle and Coinbase. That income is the toll they've collected for years, simply for holding your dollars.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 17, 2026
episodes.summary: The base layer of strategic power this week isn't a trade tool or a sanctions list. It's electrons. The clearest evidence yet that AI data-center demand has become the marginal price-setter in the largest competitive power market in the United States just landed.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 17, 2026
episodes.summary: The question hanging over this cycle is whether AI capex is peaking. TSMC just answered it, and the answer is no.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 17, 2026
episodes.summary: This week the contest shifted. It's no longer just about what agents can do — it's about how capability gets priced and packaged, and that's the thread running through everything below.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 17, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the number that reframes the whole sector this week. Databricks is raising a new strategic round at a $188 billion valuation, led by its existing investor Coatue, according to the company's own announcement. That's a term sheet, not a closed deal.

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 17, 2026
episodes.summary: The theme today is a widening gap. Capital is pricing AI-in-pharma more aggressively than ever, while the field's own leaders are quietly lowering their expectations.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 17, 2026
episodes.summary: The single biggest cross-domain story today is a discount-rate shock hitting the entire AI stack at once, and the market is not pricing it consistently.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 18, 2026
episodes.summary: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure for July 18, 2026 - re-rendered with the improved TTS pipeline (clearer pronunciation of DRAM, HBM, capex and smoother cadence).

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 18, 2026
episodes.summary: Here's the thesis for today. Molecular and antibody design is commoditizing fast. Generative structure models, protein language models, and cheminformatics agents are now available off the shelf from multiple vendors, distributed through hyperscalers, and improving on a predictable cadence.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 18, 2026
episodes.summary: The through-line this cycle is simple to state and getting expensive to ignore: value in AI products is moving off the base model and onto the layers built around it. We flagged that migration on the 17th. Today it hardens into capital structure.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 18, 2026
episodes.summary: Let's start with the paradox the tape has been circling all week: the earnings are excellent, and the market is selling anyway. FactSet's update, published July 17th, put the blended S&P 500 earnings growth rate for the second quarter at 24.7 percent, up from 22.5 percent just a week earlier.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 18, 2026
episodes.summary: For a week this briefing has been counting down to today. The GENIUS Act gave federal regulators exactly one year — until today — to finalize the rulebook for payment stablecoins. That deadline just arrived, and the rulebook didn't.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 18, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with a small procurement change that's actually a big strategic signal. Oracle is now letting enterprise customers redirect their existing Oracle Cloud Universal Credit commitments toward OpenAI model and Codex usage.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 18, 2026
episodes.summary: All week we've been building toward July 17 as Google's Gemini referendum, the moment the mid-training rebuild and the departures of senior DeepMind researchers would either be vindicated or exposed. The verdict is in, and it went against Google.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 18, 2026
episodes.summary: Start in the Gulf. On June 17th, the United States and Iran signed a memorandum establishing a ceasefire, itself a truce after the Strait of Hormuz crisis that had already pushed Brent above 126 dollars a barrel earlier this year. That memorandum lasted under three weeks.

episodes.title: The Throughline — July 18, 2026
episodes.summary: The Throughline for July 18, 2026 - the show's front-page digest, rebuilt in a new format: a one-line headline of the day, then a quick tour of every briefing (macro backdrop, the AI and compute stack, and the applied frontier), closing on the one thread worth opening two scripts for.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 19, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the premise the last few briefings on this topic have built toward. Capability stopped being a durable moat this month. A Chinese open-weight model reached frontier-adjacent scores within weeks of release, and pricing pressure has been building across the board as a result.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 19, 2026
episodes.summary: The lead today is plumbing, not price. On July 15th, the Depository Trust and Clearing Corporation began live production trades on its new tokenization service, built on a platform it calls ComposerX. Two days ago, this show could still only describe the DTCC as reportedly moving toward production testing.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 19, 2026
episodes.summary: On this feed two days ago, we told you PJM's capacity auction had made data centers the dominant cost driver in the largest power market in the country. Yesterday, we told you China had turned its rare-earth export controls from a threat into an enforced licensing regime.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 19, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with something the CEO of the world's most important foundry said out loud, because it's the tell for everything else in this cycle. On TSMC's Q2 call last week, C.C. Wei was asked point-blank about pricing power, given that memory makers are now running 86% gross margins while TSMC sits in the high 60s.

episodes.title: Robotics & Physical AI — July 19, 2026
episodes.summary: Robotics & Physical AI for July 19, 2026 - the debut of a new briefing on embodied AI. Nvidia's bid to commoditize the robot 'brain' via GR00T in Hugging Face's LeRobot; the billion-dollar valuations on independent model-layer startups; Unitree's IPO exposing compressing hardware margins; Figure's 11-month BMW deployment; and Texas fleet filings showing Waymo's 577 driverless cars to Tesla's 42.

episodes.title: Private Credit & the Corporate Credit Cycle — July 19, 2026
episodes.summary: Private Credit & the Corporate Credit Cycle for July 19, 2026 - the debut of a new briefing on the credit machine beneath the markets. The benign-default narrative cracks at the hardest-to-fake data: rising BDC non-accruals and PIK marks into Q2 earnings, LMEs that defer rather than cure distress, still-roaring CLO refinancing, a $35B AI-chip financing that shifts private credit into residual-value risk, and ESRB/RBA regulators converging on the same worry.

episodes.title: The Throughline — July 19, 2026
episodes.summary: The Throughline for July 19, 2026 - the show's front-page digest. Today's spine: capital and policy sprinting ahead of delivered reality - across nuclear-for-AI valuations, tokenized settlement, cracks in private credit's benign-default story, memory and foundry pricing, frontier-AI oversight lobbying, and robot valuations. Tours all six briefings that shipped today, including the new Private Credit and Robotics & Physical AI briefings.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: Two weeks ago, the Chinese open-weight threat was a benchmark argument: could Kimi K3 actually beat GPT-5.5 on a coding leaderboard. This week it became a capital-markets event. That's the shift to track today, because the story is no longer whether a Chinese lab can match Fable 5, Anthropic's flagship.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: On Saturday we flagged that the AI-capex duration trade was entering a repricing phase — long-dated cash flows from the AI buildout getting a harder look from a market rethinking discount rates.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the number that matters most today: 3,588. That's how many bitcoin Strategy — the company Michael Saylor built into the emblem of corporate bitcoin accumulation — sold for roughly $216 million, according to a recent company filing.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: Two days ago on this show we called the Gulf oil story a grind, not a spike, and we named the thing that would change that verdict: a firming chokepoint threat serious enough to break the market's composure. Over the weekend, it broke.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with a listing that shouldn't have needed a rescue three days later. On July 10th, SK hynix priced 177.9 million American depositary receipts at $149 apiece on Nasdaq, raising $26.5 billion. As reported, that's the largest U.S.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the base model. Moonshot AI, the Chinese lab behind the Kimi line, shipped Kimi K3 on July 16th. It's a 2.8-trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts model with a one-million-token context window, native multimodal input, and max reasoning turned on at launch.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: Let's start with the freshest, most consequential item of the week: Databricks. On July 16th, the company announced a new valuation of 188 billion dollars. That's up 40 percent from the 134 billion dollar mark it set just five months earlier, in February.

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: For weeks this feed has said the same thing: no AI-discovered molecule has yet faced a large, late-stage clinical trial, so the real verdict on this field is still years away. That changed on July 7th.

episodes.title: Robotics & Physical AI — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: The story this week isn't which lab has the smartest robot brain. It's who owns the platform underneath it.

episodes.title: Private Credit & the Corporate Credit Cycle — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday's episode argued that private credit's hardest-to-fake signals — non-accruals and payment-in-kind marks — were deteriorating before the sponsors' own self-reported valuations admitted it. Today that gap between the marks and reality has an actual quoted price attached to it, and that changes the conversation.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 20, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the macro backdrop. Strategic Power reports the US-Iran ceasefire has fully collapsed into a ninth straight night of strikes. Hormuz transit has crashed to just four vessels a day against a roughly 130-a-day baseline, and Brent broke above ninety dollars on its way to its sharpest weekly gain since spring.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: Last week the Chinese open-weight surge repriced AI-compute markets on a single premise: that sparse models mean cheap serving, so the industry needs fewer chips than everyone assumed. This week that premise hit two walls at once, and today's briefing is about what's on the other side of them.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: All week, this briefing has tracked one story turning a notch tighter each day: a discount-rate ratchet that began as a debate over when the Fed resumes cutting, hardened into a hold-with-hike-risk stance a few days ago, and then, as of Monday, became an outright repricing of the AI-capex complex.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: For a second straight week, Strategy — the company formerly known as MicroStrategy — bought zero bitcoin. Instead, it sold roughly 263.5 million dollars of its own MSTR stock, pushing its cash reserve up to 3.2 billion dollars as of July 19th.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday we led on the Gulf as a shock, Brent crude breaking above ninety dollars a barrel. Today the war is worse, not better.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index has crossed into a technical bear market. It's down more than 20% from its June 22nd record close, after rallying 105% from its March low to that peak. Since the start of July alone, the index has shed more than 18%, including its worst single week in over a year.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: For the last several days this show has been tracking a model race — who tops which coding leaderboard, whose weights are open, whose pricing undercuts whose. Today the story moves somewhere else. The freshest, best-sourced news this cycle isn't about which model wins.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with SAP, which just closed two acquisitions in under two weeks. Dremio, an open data-lakehouse specialist, closed on July 6th. Prior Labs, a German tabular-AI research lab, closed on July 17th.

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: Today the story isn't a molecule, it's the money. The hottest capital in AI biopharma has shifted from the clinic to the infrastructure and platform layer underneath it — and that spending is running well ahead of any proven clinical payoff.

episodes.title: Robotics & Physical AI — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: The freshest signal in physical AI this week isn't financial, it's operational. And the pattern that connects today's two stories is simple: the numbers that matter most are the ones operators don't volunteer.

episodes.title: Private Credit & the Corporate Credit Cycle — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: The redemption wave in private credit just hit its most symbolic point yet, and in the same breath, it broke its own narrative. Let's take those in order, because the sequence matters.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 21, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the macro backdrop. Strategic Power reports the tenth consecutive night of US strikes on Iran, and overnight Iran's Revolutionary Guard attacked a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz, forcing the crew to abandon ship.
